name: 2023 Maui Wildfire (Lahaina)
cause: Downed power lines, hurricane-force winds from Hurricane Dora
description: The Lahaina wildfire on August 8, 2023 was the deadliest US wildfire in over a century, killing 101 people and destroying the historic town of Lahaina on Maui's west coast. Despite burning only 7,000 hectares, the fire caused over $5.5 billion in damage. Hurricane Dora's distant winds drove the fire through the town at extraordinary speed, giving residents minutes to evacuate. The disaster exposed failures in emergency warning systems — outdoor sirens were never activated. Invasive fire-prone grasses that replaced former sugar cane fields fueled the rapid fire spread.
